Fix Docker build deadlock on Windows (#225)

Use event-based async reading (BeginOutputReadLine/BeginErrorReadLine)
instead of sequential synchronous ReadToEnd() calls to avoid the
classic .NET Process deadlock when both pipe buffers fill.

Docker BuildKit sends all build progress to stderr. On Windows the
stderr pipe buffer is only 4 KB, so it overflows quickly during large
image builds (e.g. multi-stage Dockerfiles with .NET SDK, Node.js,
Python). The previous code blocked on ReadToEnd(stdout) while stderr
filled up, causing Docker to block writing and deadlocking the build.

The new pattern matches Aspire's ProcessUtil approach: attach
OutputDataReceived/ErrorDataReceived event handlers that drain output
into StringBuilders on threadpool threads, then WaitForExit() safely.

Co-authored-by: Mitch Denny <mitch@mitchdeny.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(orchestrator): guard ctx usage in exception handlers to prevent NameError

If _setup_orchestrator() throws, ctx is never assigned but both except
handlers referenced ctx.finalize() and ctx.run_logger, raising NameError
and masking the original error. Initialize ctx=None before try and guard
with 'if ctx is not None' in both KeyboardInterrupt and Exception handlers.

Also moved inline 'import traceback' to top-level imports.

fix: HUAF in IccImageAnalyzer TIFF string pointers (CWE-416)

TIFFGetField returns interior pointers to directory-owned strings
(software, datetime, imagedesc). H141's TIFFSetDirectory/TIFFReadDirectory
calls TIFFFreeDirectory, freeing those strings. Subsequent strstr() on
the freed software pointer was heap-use-after-free.

Fix: copy libtiff string fields to std::string before running any
directory-walking heuristics (H141, H149). The owned copies survive
TIFFFreeDirectory safely.

Verified: 230/230 tests (was 229/230), 0 ASAN errors on the triggering
profile (ub-runtime-error-type-confusion-CIccTagEmbeddedProfile).

commit.message:
fix(security): add depth limit to RedactPII to prevent stack overflow (#44)

* fix(security): add depth limit to RedactPII to prevent stack overflow

RedactPII recursively descends into nested map[string]any values.
Pathologically deep payloads (e.g. from untrusted JSON input) could
cause a stack overflow via mutual recursion between RedactPII and
redactValue.

Changes:
- Extract redactMap() helper that carries a depth counter
- redactValue() now takes depth and returns '[TRUNCATED]' once
  maxRedactDepth (20) is exceeded
- RedactPII() API is unchanged (backward-compatible)

commit.message:
v0.1.67: 10,000x worldgen speedup via HGT tile byte cache

Root cause: SkadiElevationSource::query() called 900x per chunk (one per voxel
column), each loading 25MB HGT bytes from RocksDB TileStore — 22.5GB of I/O
per chunk causing 45-second Pass 1 times.

Fix: global (lat,lon) keyed in-process Arc<Vec<u8>> tile cache. First call for
a tile loads bytes once (TileStore or disk), subsequent 899 calls get instant
HashMap lookup (~microseconds vs ~50ms per read).

Also fixes query_cached() which had the same uncached TileStore read path.

Result: test region (18216 chunks, 24 workers) completes in ~11 seconds.
Previously took ~30 hours at 10 chunks/min.

Also removes temporary WORLDGEN_TIMING diagnostic code from terrain.rs and
the spammy 'Generating voxel columns...' println.

fix(reconciliation): catch only OSError in path resolution, return False conservatively

is_worktree_cleaned() used a bare 'except Exception: continue' when resolving
worktree paths, which silently skipped all errors including PermissionError and
OSError (e.g., MAX_PATH exceeded on Windows). This caused worktrees to be
falsely reported as cleaned, leading to orphaned worktree directories.

Changes:
- Narrow exception handler from Exception to OSError
- Log the error at WARNING level for operator visibility
- Return False (assume not cleaned) instead of continuing the loop
